key_insight: A layerwise self-adaptive PINN with dual-phase optimization better resolves high-Reynolds-number coupled Burgers’ shock fronts than standard PINNs.
engineering_relevance: This improves mesh-free simulation of steep spatio-temporal gradients in nonlinear PDEs where conventional finite difference and finite element methods become unstable or mesh-dependent.
method: Dynamically reweights physics, initial, and boundary losses during training.
Uses a dual-phase optimization strategy for more stable convergence.
Implemented in MATLAB and tested on multidimensional coupled Burgers’ equations.
evidence_signal: Numerical experiments in the abstract report lower relative L2 error than standard PINN baselines, including variants with and without L-BFGS, and better shock-front capture over time.
watch_item: Adaptive weighting and dual-stage optimization can add tuning complexity and may be sensitive to training schedule or hyperparameter choices.
paper_title: MATLAB-Based Layerwise Self-Adaptive Physics-Informed Neural Network in Applications to Multidimensional Coupled Burgers' Equations with High Reynolds Numbers
